name: TradingWizard crypto alert hub checklist
purpose: Reject naked crypto pings before they become trades.

checks.question: Does the alert say exactly what triggered?
checks.pass: Condition, asset, market and timeframe are visible.
checks.fail: The alert only says the asset moved.

checks.question: Can the trader see if the alert is stale?
checks.pass: First triggered, last triggered and stale state are visible.
checks.fail: The trader has to guess if the setup is still usable.

checks.question: Is the decision explicit?
checks.pass: BUY, SELL, WAIT or REVIEW is stated.
checks.fail: The alert creates urgency without a decision.

checks.question: Is the entry area defined?
checks.pass: Entry zone exists, or the no-entry reason is stated.
checks.fail: The alert pushes action without a plan.

checks.question: Is invalidation defined before entry?
checks.pass: Stop or invalidation condition is visible.
checks.fail: Risk gets decided after the candle moves.

checks.question: Is the target or review level defined?
checks.pass: Target, review level or exit logic is visible.

checks.question: Does the alert rank quality?
checks.pass: Confidence score and short reason are visible.

checks.question: Does route match urgency?
checks.pass: High-priority setups, review alerts and weak pings route differently.

checks.question: Is live execution blocked until review?
checks.pass: Paper mode is default before live execution.
checks.fail: The alert can jump straight to live capital.

checks.question: Can the setup be reviewed later?
checks.pass: Original alert, setup fields and later result are saved.
checks.fail: The signal disappears into chat history.

hard_rules: No win-rate claims from one receipt.
No result claim before a trade closes.
WAIT is valid when confirmation is missing.
